actionLibraryList.title: Aerobic Skiing (Straight Arms)
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand on the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees and hips slightly bent, and lean slightly forward while gripping the handles.
2.Engage your core and back, pulling the handles down until your arms swing back past your thighs, keeping the arms straight.
3.Bend and extend the knees in a fluid motion, driving the handles downward by hinging at the hips. Exhale while pulling, inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the grips to the high position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Position your feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees, and grip the handles with your hands
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: The knees bend and extend in a smooth motion, while the hips push back and descend.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your arms straight, swing them up and down, and extend your arms alongside your hips.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: As you pull the handles down towards your hips, engage the muscles of your back and lower body.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Maintain an upright posture with your back straight.\n2.Keep a slight bend in the knees and hips.\n3.Slightly lean forward while gripping the handles.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750673145702.jpg"},{"context":"1.Engage your core and back muscles, pulling the handles down until your arms swing past your thighs. Keep your arms straight throughout the movement.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750673149182.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: As you pull the handles down towards your hips, engage the muscles of your back and lower body.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your arms straight, swing them up and down, and extend your arms alongside your hips.

actionLibraryList.title: Bodyweight Alternating Forward Lunges
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start standing with your core engaged. Step one leg forward into a lunge, lowering your body until the front knee is aligned with your toes and the back knee is close to the ground without touching it. 
2.Exhale as you push back to standing through the front foot, then switch legs and repeat. Keep your torso upright and your hips facing forward. 
3.Avoid letting your front knee cave inward or extend beyond your toes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749786633555.jpg","context":"1.Align the front knee with the toes of the front foot.\n2.Lower the rear knee toward the ground without letting it touch the floor."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749786679773.jpg","context":"1.Push through the front heel to return to standing, then switch legs and repeat."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you stand up, inhale as you lower down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You should feel strong activation in your legs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid letting your knees cave inward or extend past your toes.

actionLibraryList.title: Alternating Reverse Lunge
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Keep your chest up, back straight, and core engaged.
2.Keep both knees pointing forward.
3.Step one leg back and lower until both knees are at 90°.
4.Return to standing, then alternate to the other leg.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Step one leg back into a single-leg squat.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep both knees bent at ninety degrees.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep both knees pointing straight forward.\n2.Step one leg back and lower until both knees are at 90°.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749800497307.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe naturally.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your legs and glutes.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid letting your knees go past your toes.

actionLibraryList.title: Dynamic Shoulder Circles
actionLibraryList.context: Lift your head, straighten your chest, and perform shoulder circles to the fullest range.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Touch your fingers to your shoulders and draw circles using your shoulders as the pivot point.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749524137343.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Maintain a steady breathing rhythm.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: This movement can enhance the mobility of your shoulder joint.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your body still and avoid swaying.

actionLibraryList.title: Rotator Cuff Stimulation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand and keep your upper arms parallel to the ground as you open up.                                                                                 2.The elbow joint is the fixed point for external rotation; keep your elbow at a 90-degree angle throughout the movement.                                    
3.Keep breathing and activate your rotator cuff.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Stand in a neutral position.\n2.Keep your upper arms parallel to the ground as you open up.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749717912465.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749717915966.jpg","context":"1.Using the elbow as the pivot point, perform an external rotation while maintaining a 90-degree angle at the elbow joint."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Maintain a consistent breathing rhythm.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: This movement can activate your shoulder rotator cuff muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your shoulder joints stable.

actionLibraryList.title: Dynamic Hamstring Stretch
actionLibraryList.context: Keep your upper body upright and bend forward as far as you can.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1753410285189.jpg","context":"1.Front leg straight, dorsiflex the foot.\n2.Back leg slightly bent.\n3.Shift your weight onto the back leg."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe steadily.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel the stretch in the back of your thighs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid rounding your shoulders or arching your back during the movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than hip-width.
2.Keep your shoulders back, core engaged, and knees slightly bent.
3.Begin driving your hips backward.
4.Avoid excessive knee bending or losing proper posture in your upper body.
5.Continue bending, feeling the stretch in your hamstrings.
6.Pause briefly at the bottom position, then drive your hips forward to return to standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the floor with the barbell attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Start by establishing a neutral spine while standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Hinge backward, keeping your back straight, and feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Maintain smooth breathing throughout. Inhale as you lower yourself, exhale as you rise.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Hinge at your hips to fully stretch your hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Relax your shoulders.
2.Keep your back straight and glutes engaged.
3.Slightly bend your knees.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749439326943.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your back straight.
2.Slightly bend your knees, keeping the barbell close to your body.
3.Feet hip-width apart.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749439330202.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a full stretch in your hamstrings as you hinge forward.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your knees only slightly bent.

actionLibraryList.title: Bent-Over Barbell Wide Row
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand feet shoulder-width, grip slightly wider, arms relaxed.
2.Hinge at hips with knees slightly bent, upper body nearly parallel to the floor, adjust balance.
3.Engage delts to lift the barbell to the abdomen or lower chest, elbows slightly above the back, level.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulleys to the floor with the barbell attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Hinge at your hips and keep your elbows wide as you pull the barbell up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Don't use momentum or let your torso move.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: barbell with a wide grip. Swing the barbell up until your elbows are at shoulder height.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Don't hold your breath. Exhale as you pull the bar towards you and inhale as you lower it.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your deltoids to pull the bar towards your lower chest.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Keep the shoulder blades depressed.
2.Upper body nearly parallel to the floor.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748145470980.jpg"},{"context":"1.Upper arms form approximately a 90° angle with the torso.
2.Forearms form a 90° angle with the upper arms.
3.Knees slightly bent to maintain a stable center of gravity.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748145475528.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Pause briefly at the top, feeling the back muscles engage.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Maintain a neutral spine, avoiding rounding or arching.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Front Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Rest the barbell on your front delts and cross your arms to hold it securely.
2.Lift your elbows so your upper arms are slightly above parallel to the ground.
3.Keep your head up, chest out, back straight, and core tight for stability.
4.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider, toes pointing forward or slightly outward.
5.Push your hips back to lower into the squat, keeping your back straight.
6.Maintain a steady center of balance as you rise back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulley block to the footboard end with the barbell attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Find a neutral spine while standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Do not round your back, and make sure your knees are in line with your toes when squatting.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Maintain smooth breathing, inhale as you squat, exhale as you press upward.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your quadriceps, press back into the starting stance.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider.
2.Cross your arms to hold the barbell securely with your elbows lifted.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230472738.jpg"},{"context":"1.Lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor, keeping your back straight.
2.Keep your knees aligned with your toes and avoid letting them cave inward.
3.Turn your feet slightly outward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230476871.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your quadriceps to return your body to a neutral standing position.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your back straight and align your knees with your toes as you squat down.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Barbell Shoulder Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet hip-width apart, gripping the barbell slightly wider than the hips.
2.Bring the barbell to shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows pointing outward.
3.Press the barbell overhead, extending the arms naturally.
4.At the top, slightly squeeze inward and pause briefly, focusing on shoulder contraction.
5.Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Turn the pulleys towards the platform with the barbell attachment.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't lock your elbows or thrust your hips forward when you reach the top of the motion.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you press the handle overhead, then inhale as you slowly lower it back down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Press the barbell overhead by engaging your deltoids.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Drop your shoulders.\n2.Keep your forearms vertical to the ground.\n3.Stand tall and straight.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748153078887.jpg"},{"context":"1.Extend the arms as much as possible without locking the elbows.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1759027048354.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while pressing up, inhale while lowering.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Press the barbell overhead by engaging your deltoid.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: At the top, avoid excessive hip thrust and do not lock the elbows.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Front Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Rest the barbell on your front delts and cross your arms to hold it securely.
2.Lift your elbows so your upper arms are slightly above parallel to the ground.
3.Keep your head up, chest out, back straight, and core tight for stability.
4.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider, toes pointing forward or slightly outward.
5.Push your hips back to lower into the squat, keeping your back straight.
6.Maintain a steady center of balance as you rise back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulley block to the footboard end with the barbell attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Find a neutral spine while standing.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Do not round your back, and make sure your knees are in line with your toes when squatting.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Maintain smooth breathing, inhale as you squat, exhale as you press upward.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your quadriceps, press back into the starting stance.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider.
2.Cross your arms to hold the barbell securely with your elbows lifted.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230472738.jpg"},{"context":"1.Lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor, keeping your back straight.
2.Keep your knees aligned with your toes and avoid letting them cave inward.
3.Turn your feet slightly outward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750230476871.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your quadriceps to return your body to a neutral standing position.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your back straight and align your knees with your toes as you squat down.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Barbell Shoulder Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet hip-width apart, gripping the barbell slightly wider than the hips.
2.Bring the barbell to shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows pointing outward.
3.Press the barbell overhead, extending the arms naturally.
4.At the top, slightly squeeze inward and pause briefly, focusing on shoulder contraction.
5.Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Turn the pulleys towards the platform with the barbell attachment.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't lock your elbows or thrust your hips forward when you reach the top of the motion.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you press the handle overhead, then inhale as you slowly lower it back down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Press the barbell overhead by engaging your deltoids.

actionLibraryList.title: Russian Twist
actionLibraryList.context: Keep your body stable, alternating your hands moving from side to side.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Keep your hips bent, feet off the ground, and back straight
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep your trunk stable while moving your hands alternately left and right
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Make sure your back is upright throughout the movement, do not swing your legs
